actionItems.title: Bronson's BEST Writing Assessment — Tuesday
actionItems.description: 4th Grade Florida State Test on Tuesday, March 31. 120-minute computer-based test. No electronic devices allowed. Ensure good sleep, healthy breakfast, and no electronics the night before. Do NOT schedule appointments.

actionItems.title: Kaia — Word Study Quiz Friday
actionItems.description: Potential Word Study Quiz on Friday on Soft 'c' /s/ (_ce pattern). Practice words: face, race, place, space, grace, trace. Review nightly.

actionItems.title: Teacher Appreciation Week — Daily Themes
actionItems.description: Show appreciation all week! Mon: High Fives, Tue: Letters/Notes, Wed: Dress Like Teacher/Jokes, Thu: Treats (fave snacks), Fri: Gifts. Ms. Cuccinello loves tulips, chocolate chip cookies, and Diet Coke.

actionItems.title: Kaia — Green Meadows Field Trip April 17
actionItems.description: 2nd Grade field trip to Green Meadows Petting Farm on April 17. Mark your calendar and watch for permission slip details.

actionItems.title: Check Canvas & Skyward for Missing Work
actionItems.description: New 4th grading period has begun. Check Canvas and Skyward for any incomplete assignments from the previous grading period.

importantDates.title: Bronson — BEST Writing Assessment
importantDates.description: 4th Grade Florida State Test. 120-minute computer-based test. No electronic devices allowed. Ensure good sleep and healthy breakfast. Do NOT schedule appointments.

importantDates.title: Teacher Appreciation — High Fives Day
importantDates.description: Show appreciation by giving teachers and staff a HIGH FIVE! All week: Mon-High Fives, Tue-Letters, Wed-Dress Like Teacher/Jokes, Thu-Treats, Fri-Gifts.

importantDates.title: Teacher Appreciation — Letters Day
importantDates.description: Write kind notes or draw pictures for your teachers today.

importantDates.title: Teacher Appreciation — Dress Like Teacher / Jokes
importantDates.description: Dress like your teacher or tell them a joke!

importantDates.title: Teacher Appreciation — Treats Day
importantDates.description: Bring your teacher or a staff member their favorite snack or drink. Ms. Cuccinello loves: chocolate chip cookies, chips & salsa, Diet Coke, iced caramel latte with coconut milk.

importantDates.title: Teacher Appreciation — Gifts Day
importantDates.description: Bring your teacher a gift card or item off their Amazon wishlist. Ms. Cuccinello's favorites: tulips, chocolate chip cookies, chips & salsa.

importantDates.title: Kaia — Word Study Quiz (Soft 'c' /s/)
importantDates.description: Potential Word Study Quiz on Soft 'c' /s/ (_ce pattern). Practice words: face, race, place, space, grace, trace.

importantDates.description: 2nd Grade field trip to Green Meadows Petting Farm. Watch for permission slip details.

importantDates.description: 15th Annual Gala at West Orange Country Club, 6–10 PM. Mardi Gras Masquerade theme. Tickets $50/person.

importantDates.description: OCPS Teacher Professional Day. Students have the day off. Teachers participate in professional development.

importantDates.description: End of Fourth Marking Period. Last day of the 2025-2026 school year for students.

messages.subject: Teacher Appreciation Week & BEST Writing Prep
messages.summary: Teacher Appreciation Week begins Monday! Themed days: High Fives (Mon), Letters (Tue), Dress Like Teacher/Jokes (Wed), Treats (Thu), Gifts (Fri). Ms. Cuccinello's favorites: tulips, chocolate chip cookies, chips & salsa, Diet Coke, iced caramel latte with coconut milk. Also: BEST Writing Assessment is Tuesday March 31 — ensure Bronson gets good sleep and a healthy breakfast.

messages.subject: Teacher Appreciation Week & Upcoming Field Trip
messages.summary: Teacher Appreciation Week begins Monday! Show Ms. Smith some love with the daily themes. Looking ahead: 2nd Grade field trip to Green Meadows Petting Farm on April 17. Watch for permission slip details.

assignments.subject: Writing
assignments.title: BEST Writing Practice
assignments.description: Practice persuasive writing in preparation for Tuesday's state assessment. Review your thesis, supporting details, and conclusion structure.

assignments.description: Read independently for 20 minutes each night and have a parent sign the reading log.

assignments.description: Per Ms. Cuccinello: check Canvas and Skyward for any missing or incomplete assignments from Q3 as the 4th grading period begins.

assignments.description: Practice Soft 'c' /s/ words using the _ce pattern (e.g., face, race, place, space). Quiz may be on Friday.

actionItems.title: Prepare Bronson for BEST Writing Assessment
actionItems.description: State test on Tuesday, March 31 — 120-minute computer-based exam. Ensure good sleep, healthy breakfast, and no electronics the night before. Practice persuasive writing this week.

actionItems.title: Check Canvas & Skyward for Missing Work
actionItems.description: Per Ms. Cuccinello's March 9-13 newsletter, all work was due Thursday March 13. Check Canvas and Skyward for any incomplete assignments before the 4th grading period begins.

actionItems.title: Rain Gear Needed Wed–Fri
actionItems.description: Afternoon and morning showers expected Wednesday through Friday (34-44% chance). Pack rain jackets and umbrellas for school drop-off and pickup.

actionItems.title: PTO Teacher Appreciation Week Starts March 30
actionItems.description: Teacher Appreciation Week begins next Monday. Start planning gifts or notes for Ms. Cuccinello and Ms. Smith.

actionItems.title: Expect Fresh Newsletters This Sunday
actionItems.description: No newsletters were sent during Spring Break. Both Ms. Cuccinello and Ms. Smith typically send weekly updates on Sunday evenings.

weather.dressSuggestion: Light, breathable clothing — shorts and t-shirt weather. Don't forget sunscreen!
Light layers. Comfortable for shorts and a light top. Sunglasses recommended.
Pack a light rain jacket or umbrella for afternoon pickup. Light clothing otherwise.
Rain jacket and umbrella recommended. Avoid open-toed shoes. Light layers.
Morning rain likely — bring umbrella for drop-off. Should clear up by afternoon.

messages.subject: Week of March 9-13 Newsletter & BEST Writing Prep
messages.summary: Last newsletter before Spring Break covered the week of March 9-13. Key highlights: All work was due Thursday March 13 — check Canvas and Skyward for any missing assignments. Field Day was held March 12 (wear class shirt, bring water bottle, towel, change of clothes). Spring Break runs March 14-22. School resumes March 23 with the 4th Grading Period. The BEST Writing Assessment is March 31 (120-min computer-based state test).

messages.subject: BEST Writing Assessment Preparation
messages.summary: Reminder about the 4th Grade BEST Writing Assessment on Tuesday, March 31. This is a 120-minute computer-based state test. Students should get good sleep, eat a healthy breakfast, and avoid electronics the night before. No personal electronics allowed during testing.

messages.subject: Week of March 9-13 Newsletter
messages.summary: Last newsletter before Spring Break. Phonics focus: Diphthongs (ou, ow, oi, oy, au, aw). ELA: Assessing similes, idioms, alliteration, rhyme schemes & themes in poems; context clues and word relationships. Math: Compare Numbers Using Place Value. Science: Human body parts and basic functions. Social Studies: Native American Cultures quiz. Assessments scheduled throughout the week. Homework: Math pages 467-468 (Mon), 469-470 (Tue), Performance Task pages 475-476 (Wed). Wednesdays are School Spirit days.

messages.subject: Blooket Review Games Shared
messages.summary: Ms. Smith shared Blooket review links for students to practice at home: Math (Numbers to 1000), Science (Human Body Parts), and Social Studies review game via TCI on OCPS Launchpad.

messages.subject: Spring Break Weekly — March 23-27 Newsletter
messages.summary: Students averaged 98% on science test and 90% on word study test before break. This week: Phonics focus is Soft 'c' /s/ (_ce). Daily Math HW pages 153-162 (Add/Subtract with Numbers to 1,000). Potential Word Study Quiz Friday. Money Madness Open Book Test Friday. Media Center Thursday — send in library books. Show and tell / tech time Monday. After school support available Friday until 4pm. Field trip to Green Meadows Petting Farm planned for 4/17.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Wishing You a Safe and Restful Spring Break
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Thank you for the important role you play each day in supporting your child's education. As we begin spring break, I want to wish you and your family a safe, happy, and relaxing week. When students return to school on Monday, March 23, we will begin the final nine weeks of the school year — an exciting time filled with important learning experiences, milestones, and celebrations.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Field Day Recap & Reminders
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Field Day was held on March 12 with fun activities, inflatables, and a DJ. Only pre-registered volunteers were permitted on campus for security. Thank you to all parents who signed up to help make it a success!

schoolDistrictComms.subject: 15th Annual DEF Silent Auction & Gala — April 18
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The 15th Annual DEF Silent Auction & Gala is Saturday, April 18 from 6-10 PM at the West Orange Country Club. Theme: Mardi Gras Masquerade. Tickets are $50/person (includes dinner, 2 drink tickets, and auction access). All funds go to the Dolphin Education Fund for classroom supplies, arts programs, PE equipment, and more. Purchase tickets at tinyurl.com/DEF-Gala.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Give Education Day — Take the $1 Pledge
schoolDistrictComms.from: Foundation for Orange County Public Schools
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The Foundation for OCPS is asking families to take the $1 Pledge at the Give Education Day website. Funds support literacy enrichment, STEM and arts access, college and career readiness, scholarships, and professional development for teachers.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Mondays with Maria — Weekly Superintendent Update
schoolDistrictComms.summary: This week's Mondays with Maria featured a visit to Colonial High School with new Deputy Superintendent Jose Martinez, who previously served as the school's principal. Colonial High students shared what they love most about their campus. Available as a video and podcast.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Dolphin Digest — Week of March 2, 2026
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The principal's weekly update highlighted an incredibly busy and rewarding week including All-County Music performances and neon dance activities. Students continue to shine in everything they do. The newsletter also covered upcoming events and school community updates.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: Yearbook Price Increase — Order Before March 1
schoolDistrictComms.summary: Yearbook prices increased after March 1, 2026. Families were encouraged to order before the deadline to get the lower price. Yearbooks capture all the memories from this school year.

schoolDistrictComms.subject: INFORM: OCPS — District 4 News: Black History Month 2026
schoolDistrictComms.summary: The District 4 newsletter covered Black History Month recognition events and second semester updates across Orange County Public Schools. The newsletter highlighted community celebrations and student achievements throughout the district.

highlights.title: Community Dance Recap
highlights.body: Thank you for your wonderful support at our school dance on February 27 — it was a joy to see our community come together.

highlights.title: Student Spotlight: Black History Month
highlights.body: Haven Bennett was awarded 1st place at the Black History Essay Contest for West Orange County, representing Lake Whitney!

highlights.title: Book Fair Success
highlights.body: The Scholastic Book Fair was a huge success! Thank you to Mrs. Dudek and all the volunteers who made it possible.

highlights.title: Field Day — March 12
highlights.body: Field Day is approaching! All guests must be ADDitions-approved volunteers to attend. Ensure your clearance is active and sign up for a volunteer shift.

highlights.title: School of Excellence
highlights.body: Lake Whitney has been recognized as a 2024-25 School of Excellence by the Florida Department of Education!
